title: gh-146613: Fix re-entrant use-after-free in itertools._grouper
category: re-entrant use-after-free (itertools _grouper compares borrowed keys across a user __eq__)
crash_site: SIGSEGV / use-after-free while iterating an itertools.groupby grouper whose keys have a custom __eq__: the C code compares igo->tgtkey against gbo->currkey, the user __eq__ re-enters the grouper/groupby and mutates currkey (dropping its last ref), and the comparison or the next step then dereferences the freed key. Crash in the grouper step, driven by re-entrancy in the comparison.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the user-defined __eq__ (the TRIGGER, not the bug); the parent groupby_next; PyObject_RichCompareBool (correct). The bug is _grouper_next comparing BORROWED tgtkey/currkey without holding refs across the compare.
ground_truth.fix_summary: _grouper_next() passed igo->tgtkey and gbo->currkey (borrowed) straight into PyObject_RichCompareBool. A user __eq__ can re-enter the iterator and mutate gbo->currkey during the compare, freeing the object the C code still points at -> use-after-free. Fix: take strong snapshots (tgtkey = Py_NewRef(igo->tgtkey); currkey = Py_NewRef(gbo->currkey)) around the comparison and Py_DECREF them after.

admission.note: monogram+haiku vs baseline+haiku discrimination. HARD: trigger is a user __eq__, but the bug is _grouper_next comparing borrowed key pointers across that re-entrant call. Live decoy = blaming __eq__ or the compare. Authored from PR #147962 fix diff (Py_NewRef snapshots of tgtkey/currkey). NOTE: scaffold root_cause_file was the Misc/NEWS.d changelog .rst — corrected to Modules/itertoolsmodule.c.

provenance: https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/147962 — gh-146613 re-entrant UAF in itertools._grouper (merged 2026-04-02); fix commit fc7a188fe70a; base c1a4112c225e. Root cause + fix from the commit diff (_grouper_next Py_NewRef snapshots).
